what_it_does: Acceptance test for the R1 backend current-locus feature: it verifies that a Suite instance holds an in-memory current ui:// locus, that the locus is set inside _chat_context from focus.selected, and that the getter returns the expected value under last-wins, non-clobber, mixed-focus, and unregistered-but-well-formed cases, while preserving existing per-request focus context blocks.
observations: Drives Suite._chat_context directly, avoiding any completion model
Uses a real FsStore, NodeRegistry, and Suite with discovered nodes
Creates a small graph with constant and uppercase nodes
Preconditions verify that ADDR_A and ADDR_B are genuinely present in suite.UI_REGISTRY
Tests that current_locus() starts as None
Tests that a ui:// focus turn sets the locus
Tests that a subsequent new ui:// focus updates the locus (last-wins)
Tests that node-id-only and no-focus turns do not clobber the remembered ui:// locus
Tests multi-ui:// select uses the last address
Tests mixed node-id + ui:// focus still sets the ui:// locus
Tests an unregistered but well-formed ui:// address is still stored
Tests that per-request co-presence and INDICATING blocks are still injected
Uses a global PASS counter incremented by a local check() helper
standouts: Explicitly tests in-memory (non-store-backed) backend locus persistence
Treats _chat_context as the deterministic set-point for the locus
Documents design rationale in the module docstring (single live operator, restart-survival, no store consumer)
Distinguishes clobber semantics precisely: last-wins for ui://, no clobber for node-id/no focus
Accepts unregistered-but-well-formed ui:// addresses as valid loci
conventions: Heavy explanatory docstring with design decisions and seam references
Uses real registry rows rather than fabricated handles
Uses a lightweight local check() helper instead of a full test framework
Directly exercises a private method for deterministic testing
concerns: Coupled to the private _chat_context implementation; renaming or signature changes break the test
String assertions on rendered context ('OPERATOR'S CURRENT FOCUS', 'INDICATING') are brittle
Global PASS counter is not thread-safe and is unusual for an acceptance test
Does not exercise the malformed ui:// raise path; only comments on it
Assumes UI_REGISTRY row structure and address construction logic
notes: An acceptance test that proves the backend remembers the operator's current ui:// locus across turns while keeping the existing per-request focus path intact.
questions: What are I1 and R2, and where are they defined?
What is the exact contract and location of Suite._chat_context?
Is current_locus() already implemented, or is this test driving its addition?
How is suite.UI_REGISTRY populated and what guarantees its row ordering/structure?
What should happen if focus.selected contains both a malformed ui:// and a valid one?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file: it tests that the backend holds, sets, updates, and reads a current ui:// locus with the documented semantics.
intention_signals: To establish that the Suite can remember the operator's current ui:// locus across chat turns in memory, using the existing _chat_context focus-handling code as the set-point, so that later resolution (R2) can read it live.
novelty: The deliberate choice of an in-memory Suite attribute for locus persistence, with explicit non-clobber and last-wins rules tested as first-class behavior.
contribution: An executable acceptance specification for backend current-locus behavior, including getter semantics, update rules, mixed-focus handling, and preservation of per-request focus context injection.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance test for R1 that verifies a Suite instance holds, sets, updates, and returns an in-memory current ui:// locus via _chat_context, with last-wins and non-clobber semantics, while preserving per-request focus blocks.
intention_for_embedding: To prove the backend can remember the operator's current ui:// locus across chat turns using the existing _chat_context set-point without store persistence.

symbols.does: Local assertion helper that increments a global pass counter and prints an ok line.
symbols.description: check(label, cond) is a lightweight test helper used throughout the file. It asserts cond with a labeled failure message, increments the global PASS counter, and prints a success marker for each passing check.
